NOTICE OF AND COURT'S NOMINATION OF PROPOSED ARBITRATORS

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER NAOMI PEARCE NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION COMPELLING ARBITRATION AND APPOINTING ARBITRATOR. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITION COMPELLING ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. THIS DISPUTE IS SUBJECT TO A VALID ARBITRATION AGREEMENT AND NEITHER OF THE EXCEPTIONS PROVIDED IN CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1281.2 APPLIES. DEFENSES; INCLUDING STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS, WILL BE ADJUDICATED BY THE ARBITRATOR. APPOINTMENT OF THE ARBITRATOR IS GOVERNED BY CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1281.6. HAVING RECEIVED PETITIONER'S LIST, THE COURT ORDERS RESPONDENTS TO SUPPLY LISTS OF ARBITRATORS NO LATER THAN CLOSE OF BUSINESS JULY 31, 2009. THE COURT WILL NOMINATE FIVE PERSONS FROM THE LISTS SUBMITTED. WITHIN FIVE DAYS OF RECEIPT OF THE NOTICE OF THE NOMINEES FROM THE COURT, THE PARTIES MAY JOINTLY SELECT AN ARBITRATOR. IF THE PARTIES FAIL TO SELECT AN ARBITRATOR WITHIN THE FIVE DAY PERIOD, THE COURT SHALL APPOINT AN ARBITRATOR FROM THE NOMINEES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Continued Hearing On Petition Compelling Arbitration And Appointing Arbitrator
Off Calendar Jul-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER NAOMI PEARCE Notice of Hearing on Petition Compelling Arbitration and Appointing Arbitrator. Appearances: Ethan Schatz, Esq. of Weisberg & Miller (415) 686-8472, appearing for Petitioner and moving party; Tim Lundeen, Pro Per respondent, appearing and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petition compelling arbitration is granted. This dispute is subject to a valid arbitration agreement and neither of the exceptions provided in Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.2 applies. Defenses; including statute of limitations, will be adjudicated by the arbitrator. Appointment of the arbitrator is governed by Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.6. Having received Petitioner's list, the court orders respondents to supply lists of arbitrators no later than close of business July 31, 2009. The court will nominate five persons from the lists submitted. Within five days of receipt of the notice of the nominees from the court, the parties may jointly select an arbitrator. If the parties fail to select an arbitrator within the five day period, the court shall appoint an arbitrator from the nominees. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
